title: Gemäldegalerie Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **The Gemäldegalerie is Berlin's old masters gallery and its emptiest world-class museum.** 72 rooms, about 1,250 paintings from the 13th to 18th century, a whole hall of Rembrandts, two Vermeers, a Caravaggio and Bruegel's Dutch Proverbs, ten minutes from Potsdamer Platz at the Kulturforum.

**Do not try to see all 72 rooms.** The 18 main halls run in one numbered loop around the central hall. Pick up to five target paintings with the planner in this guide, walk the loop once, and treat everything in between as a bonus.

**Tickets are 14 euros, concessions 7, under 18 free, closed Mondays.** The 22 euro Kulturforum day ticket adds the neighbouring exhibitions. Berlin's free Museum Sunday ended on 1 December 2024, so ignore older posts promising free first Sundays.

**The neighbours are worth the afternoon.** The Neue Nationalgalerie shows Brancusi until 9 August 2026 and is free on the first Thursday evening of each month; the Philharmonie runs free Wednesday lunch concerts from September to June.

**It pairs cleanly with the free tour.** My walking tour ends near Hackescher Markt, and the S-Bahn runs from there to Potsdamer Platz in under ten minutes: historic centre in the morning, Rembrandts almost to yourself in the afternoon.
